metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1414
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors studied the role of calcineurin A beta subunit in primary hippocampal cultures from WT and KO mice by using the overexpression of the pH sensitive probe synaptopHluoring, which allows the monitoring of the synaptic vesicle cycle. In particular, they studied the role of the protein in endocytosis. Authors observed that the decay rate in boutons with lower peak responses was significantly larger in the absence of CNA beta that in WT neurons. This was reproduced using the same experimental approach but using milder stimulation, which showed a clear impairment in endocytosis in the absence of CNA beta as compared to control neurons.. Experimental description: Experiments were performed in mouse primary cultures.

Information regarding the animals (from M&M):

Calcineurin A / and A / mice were pro- vided by Dr. J. L. Gooch (Emory University School of Medicine, Atlanta, GA) (Zhang et al., 1996) and J. D. Molkentin (Bueno et al., 2002), respec- tively. Calcineurin A / and A / mice were obtained by heterozygous breeding using standard mouse husbandry procedures. Mouse genotypes were determined by PCR with primers described previously (Gooch et al., 2004).
